 {"id":"git_safety_guard-14xe","title":"[PACK] cloud.aws: ECR + CloudWatch Logs destructive patterns","description":"# AWS pack gaps\n\n## Scope\nExtend cloud.aws pack to include destructive operations for ECR and CloudWatch Logs.\n\n## Destructive patterns (examples)\n- aws ecr delete-repository\n- aws ecr batch-delete-image\n- aws ecr delete-lifecycle-policy\n- aws logs delete-log-group\n- aws logs delete-log-stream\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- [ ] Patterns added with reasons + tests\n- [ ] Keywords updated if needed (ecr, logs)\n- [ ] E2E pack cases added","notes":"Implemented in src/packs/cloud/aws.rs: added keywords ecr/logs and destructive patterns for ecr delete-repository/batch-delete-image/delete-lifecycle-policy and logs delete-log-group/delete-log-stream; added unit test coverage.\n\nQuality gates: cargo fmt --check failed due to pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated files; cargo check --all-targets passed with warnings; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings failed due to pre-existing clippy issues in src/packs/test_helpers.rs and src/evaluator.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:40.636186362-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:04.071766261-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:04.071766261-05:00","close_reason":"Patterns + tests added; gates blocked by unrelated pre-existing format/clippy issues"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-16f","title":"[CATEGORY] loadbalancer.* - Load Balancer Pack Suite","description":"# Load Balancer Pack Suite

## Overview
Load balancers control traffic routing. Misconfiguration can cause outages or route traffic incorrectly. Backend removal drops active connections.

## Packs to Implement

### loadbalancer.nginx
- **Why Critical**: nginx is the most popular reverse proxy. Config changes require reload and can cause downtime.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `nginx -s stop` - Stops nginx entirely
  - `systemctl stop nginx`
  - Editing nginx.conf with upstream removal
  - Deleting files in /etc/nginx/ configs
- **Safe Patterns**: `nginx -t` (config test), `nginx -T` (dump config)

### loadbalancer.haproxy
- **Why Critical**: HAProxy handles high-traffic load balancing. Backend changes affect live traffic.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `systemctl stop haproxy`
  - Config changes removing backends
  - `haproxy -f` with bad config
- **Safe Patterns**: `haproxy -c` (config check)

### loadbalancer.elb
- **Why Critical**: AWS ELB/ALB/NLB handle cloud traffic routing. Target group changes affect service availability.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer`
  - `aws elbv2 delete-target-group`
  - `aws elbv2 deregister-targets`
  - `aws elb delete-load-balancer` (classic)
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe-`, `list-`

### loadbalancer.traefik
- **Why Critical**: Traefik is popular in container environments. Dynamic config changes affect routing immediately.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Container/pod deletion affecting Traefik
  - Config removal from dynamic config directory
- **Safe Patterns**: `/api/`, `/dashboard/` endpoints

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major load balancer tools covered
- [ ] Config file operations detected
- [ ] Service stop/restart operations handled
- [ ] Cloud provider LB APIs covered","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:55.05887554-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:29:40.61994914-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:29:40.61994914-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-0rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:46.101876708-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jbg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:46.131800716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-tff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:57.329681659-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-eoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:57.36474505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.651826103-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.697069589-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.743970184-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.79018607-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.836981135-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-16f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.885231549-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","title":"Rule-Level Metrics and Analytics","description":"# Epic: Rule-Level Metrics and Analytics

## Priority Score: 550/1000 (Fifth Priority)

## Background and Rationale

Users and maintainers need to understand which patterns are effective and which are noisy. This enables data-driven pattern tuning and builds confidence that DCG is providing value.

Currently DCG tracks history but does not aggregate by rule_id for analytics. This epic adds rule-level metrics showing hit counts, allowlist override rates, and effectiveness scores.

## Example Output

dcg stats --rules

Shows:
  Rule                        Hits   Allowed   Noise%   Last Hit
  ----------------------------------------------------------------
  git.reset-hard               47        3      6.4%    2h ago
  filesystem.chmod-777         23       18     78.3%    1d ago  [NOISY]
  docker.system-prune          12        0      0.0%    3h ago
  
  Recommendations:
  - Consider reviewing filesystem.chmod-777 (78% allowlist rate)
  - git.reset-hard is working effectively (low override rate)

## Technical Approach

1. Ensure rule_id is captured in every history entry
2. Add analytics queries grouped by rule_id
3. Calculate metrics: hit count, allowlist rate, time since last hit
4. Identify noisy patterns (high allowlist rate)
5. Generate recommendations for tuning

## Metrics Calculated

- Total hits per rule
- Allowlist override count and percentage
- Time since last trigger
- Trend (increasing/decreasing over time)
- Effectiveness score (hits - allowlist overrides)

## Key Files

- src/history/schema.rs - Ensure rule_id field populated
- src/history/mod.rs - Add analytics query methods
- src/stats.rs - Rule-level aggregation
- src/cli.rs - dcg stats --rules command

## Success Criteria

- Every denial logs rule_id in history
- Stats view shows per-rule breakdown
- Noisy patterns flagged automatically
- Recommendations actionable and clear

## Dependencies

This epic provides foundation for Auto-Suggested Allowlists epic, which needs rule_id tracking to cluster commands effectively.

## Note on Existing Implementation

DCG already has some pattern effectiveness tracking (PatternEffectiveness, PackEffectivenessAnalysis). This epic extends and surfaces that functionality in a user-friendly CLI view.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:05:06.219714293-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:05:06.219714293-05:00"}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.1","title":"[Metrics] Reuse existing history structs (PatternEffectiveness, etc.)","description":"# Task: Reuse existing history structs (PatternEffectiveness, etc.)

## Purpose
Avoid duplicate analytics paths by explicitly reusing existing history types
(e.g., PatternEffectiveness, PackEffectivenessAnalysis, PatternStat).

## Requirements
- Inventory current history analytics structs
- Decide which ones to reuse vs extend
- Document mapping from new rule-metrics needs to existing types

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rule-metrics design references existing structs
- No duplicate or competing analytics pipelines introduced

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.1","title":"[Metrics] Reuse existing history structs (PatternEffectiveness, etc.)","description":"# Task: Reuse existing history structs (PatternEffectiveness, etc.)

## Purpose
Avoid duplicate analytics paths by explicitly reusing existing history types
(e.g., PatternEffectiveness, PackEffectivenessAnalysis, PatternStat).

## Requirements
- Inventory current history analytics structs
- Decide which ones to reuse vs extend
- Document mapping from new rule-metrics needs to existing types

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rule-metrics design references existing structs
- No duplicate or competing analytics pipelines introduced
","notes":"## Inventory of Existing Analytics Structs

### Core Structs for Reuse

1. **PatternEffectiveness** (schema.rs:2402)
   - Fields: pattern, pack_id, total_triggers, denied_count, bypassed_count, bypass_rate
   - Reuse: Extend with optional `rule_id` field for stable pattern identification
   - This is the primary struct for per-rule analytics

2. **PatternStat** (schema.rs:248)
   - Fields: name, count, pack_id
   - Reuse: Simple summary counts; can be grouped by rule_id in queries
   - Used for top-N pattern lists

3. **HistoryStats** (schema.rs:325)
   - Fields: period_days, total_commands, outcomes, block_rate, top_patterns, etc.
   - Reuse: Wrapper for aggregated stats; top_patterns uses PatternStat
   - No changes needed; queries can group by rule_id internally

4. **PackEffectivenessAnalysis** (schema.rs:2471)
   - Fields: high_value_patterns, potentially_aggressive (both Vec<PatternEffectiveness>)
   - Reuse: Already uses PatternEffectiveness; benefits from rule_id extension

## Mapping to Rule-Level Metrics

| New Metric Need | Existing Struct | Approach |
|-----------------|-----------------|----------|
| Per-rule trigger counts | PatternEffectiveness | Add rule_id field |
| Per-rule bypass rates | PatternEffectiveness | Already has bypass_rate |
| Rule effectiveness trends | PatternEffectiveness + timestamps | Query by rule_id with time grouping |
| Top rules by volume | PatternStat | Query groups by rule_id |

## Recommended Changes

1. Add optional `rule_id: Option<String>` to PatternEffectiveness
2. Update effectiveness queries to use rule_id for grouping when available
3. Keep backward compat: fall back to pack_id:pattern_name if rule_id is NULL

## No New Structs Required
The existing PatternEffectiveness struct covers rule-level metrics needs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:21:18.574744451-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:21:42.094279024-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:21:42.094279024-05:00","close_reason":"Design complete: documented existing structs (PatternEffectiveness, PatternStat, HistoryStats, PackEffectivenessAnalysis) and mapping to rule-level metrics needs. PatternEffectiveness is the primary struct to extend with rule_id field.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:21:18.57611411-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.2","title":"[Metrics] CLI output format + JSON option for rule metrics","description":"# Task: CLI output format + JSON option for rule metrics

## Purpose
Provide both human-readable and machine-readable output for rule-level metrics.

## Requirements
- Pretty table output in terminal
- JSON output suitable for CI or dashboards
- Stable field names for rule_id, pack_id, counts, allowlist rate

## Acceptance Criteria
- `dcg stats --rules --format json` produces valid JSON
- Pretty output includes totals and top N rules

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.3","title":"[Metrics] Unit tests for rule metrics queries","description":"# Task: Unit tests for rule metrics queries

## Purpose
Validate correctness of rule-level analytics (counts, allowlist rate, severity
aggregation) with deterministic fixtures.

## Requirements
- Fixture history DB or in-memory data
- Tests for query correctness
- Ensure stats match expected totals

- Clear failure diagnostics

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.4","title":"[Metrics] E2E tests for stats --rules (pretty + JSON)","description":"# Task: E2E tests for stats --rules (pretty + JSON)

## Purpose
Ensure the CLI output for rule metrics is stable and correct in both formats.

## Requirements
- Fixture data with known rule counts
- Validate pretty output includes totals and top N
- Validate JSON output schema and values
- Verbose logging on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests pass consistently
- Output formats are deterministic

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","title":"[TASK] Performance regression testing for packs","description":"# Performance Regression Testing for Packs

## Scope
Ensure adding new packs doesn't degrade dcg performance.

## Deliverables

### 1. Performance Benchmark Suite
Create `benches/pack_performance.rs`:
```rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, Criterion};

fn bench_single_pack_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark each pack individually
}

fn bench_all_packs_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark with all packs enabled
}

fn bench_pattern_matching(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark regex pattern matching specifically
}
```

### 2. Performance Budgets
Define maximum acceptable times:
- Single pack evaluation: < 500µs
- All packs evaluation: < 5ms
- Regex pattern match: < 100µs per pattern
- Total hook execution: < 10ms

### 3. CI Performance Tracking
- Run benchmarks on every PR
- Compare against baseline
- Fail PR if regression > 10%
- Track performance over time (grafana/dashboard)

### 4. Performance Logging
When DEBUG enabled:
- Log time per pack
- Log time per pattern
- Identify slow patterns
- Suggest optimizations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark suite covers all performance-critical paths
- [ ] Budgets defined and enforced in CI
- [ ] Performance tracked over time
- [ ] Slow patterns flagged for optimization","notes":"Context (perf reality check):
- Current process-per-command baseline (release) is ~173–179ms p50 for normal hook invocations because pack registry initialization compiles regexes.
- Criterion benches (in-process) are still useful, but they do NOT capture process startup + per-invocation regex compilation cost.

Plan adjustments:
- Treat `git_safety_guard-64dc` (lazy registry + lazy compilation) as the prerequisite that makes pack performance budgets meaningful.
- After startup costs are eliminated, criterion benches can focus on steady-state evaluation cost (sanitize/normalize/pack matching).
- Add a process-level harness (JSON baseline + diff) via `git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3` so CI can gate on p95/p99 for the allow path.

Budget note:
- The "Total hook execution < 10ms" budget is a good target but should be enforced only after the startup refactor lands, otherwise it will fail constantly and become noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:34:15.054793749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:39:21.26259381-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:39:21.26259381-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Updated heredoc_perf.rs to include cicd and safe.cleanup packs in worst-case scenarios, verified performance remains within budgets.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:54:17.803032297-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:54:22.89206455-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qyav","title":"[BUG][P1] pack_aware_quick_reject misses rm when args are /tmp or quoted ","description":"Regression: golden rm parity test shows allow for commands that should be denied (e.g. rm -r -f "/foo"). Suspect pack_aware_quick_reject_with_normalized fails to detect 'rm' keyword in executable spans when targets are /tmp or quoted , causing Step 4 quick-reject in evaluator to allow early. Fix quick-reject keyword gating to be conservative for rm/gits command words and add unit tests covering /tmp + quoted TMPDIR variants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:18:30.416258064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:04:36.386761667-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:04:36.386761667-05:00","close_reason":"Fix quick-reject + normalization for rm /tmp and quoted TMPDIR variants; added regression tests; full gates + e2e green."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r85z","title":"Preserve env assignments with command substitution","description":"strip_env currently skips env assignments even when they include backticks or  command substitution, which can hide destructive commands inside the assignment. Detect inline code in assignment tokens and stop skipping them; add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:43:00.958592452-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:44:41.048502315-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T12:44:41.048502315-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e","title":"Visual Explanations for Pattern Blocks","description":"# Epic: Visual Explanations for Pattern Blocks

## Priority Score: 750/1000 (Second Highest)

## Background and Rationale

This feature complements span highlighting by answering the deeper question: WHY does this pattern exist?

Users need to understand not just WHAT triggered the block, but WHY it matters. This transforms DCG from an obstacle into a teacher, building trust and helping users make informed decisions about allowlisting.

## Example Output

When showing a denial, include contextual explanation:

  Pattern: git.reset-hard
  Why: git reset --hard destroys uncommitted changes permanently.
       There is no way to recover work that has not been committed.
  
  Suggestion: Use git stash to save changes first, or git reset --soft
              to preserve changes in the working directory.

## Technical Approach

1. Add optional explanation field to DestructivePattern struct
2. Write explanations for all core patterns (approximately 30-40 patterns)
3. Create suggestion templates for common remediation steps
4. Integrate into hook denial output alongside span highlighting
5. Surface in dcg explain output

## Key Files

- src/packs/mod.rs - Add explanation field to DestructivePattern
- src/packs/core/*.rs - Add explanations to each pattern
- src/hook.rs - Display explanations in denial output
- src/trace.rs - Include in explain output

## Content Strategy

Explanations should include:
- What the command does (destructive action)
- Why it is dangerous (consequences)
- What could go wrong (scenarios)
- Safe alternatives (suggestions)

## Success Criteria

- Every core pattern has an explanation
- Explanations are concise but informative (2-4 sentences)
- At least one safe alternative suggested per pattern
- Documentation links where applicable

## Synergy with Epic 1

This works together with span highlighting - users see WHAT matched and understand WHY it matters. The combination is more powerful than either alone.","notes":"All packs must receive explanations; tasks under this epic partition the workload but do not narrow scope. Epic is not complete until explanations exist for core + all non-core packs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:33.031740999-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:52:29.487123468-05:00"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.1","title":"[Explain] Explanation content style guide","description":"# Task: Explanation content style guide

## Purpose
Provide a consistent writing standard for explanations so tone, length, and
structure are predictable and professional.

## Requirements
- Short, neutral, factual language
- Avoid scaremongering or moralizing
- 2–4 sentences max unless complexity requires more
- Include consequence + safer alternative (when applicable)
- Prefer deterministic guidance over subjective phrasing

## Deliverables
- A short style guide in `docs/explanations.md` (or similar)
- Examples of **good vs bad** explanation text
- Recommended structure template

## Acceptance Criteria
- Guide is concise and usable by anyone editing pack patterns
- Example explanations cover git + filesystem + one non-core pack

## Notes
This guide is a dependency for writing large volumes of explanation text.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.2","title":"[Explain] Fallback explanation for patterns without explicit text","description":"# Task: Fallback explanation for patterns without explicit text

## Purpose
Not all patterns will have bespoke explanations immediately. Provide a safe,
clear fallback to avoid empty explanation sections and keep UX consistent.

## Requirements
- Fallback should mention the matched pack + pattern name
- Explain that the command matched a destructive pattern and point to
  `dcg explain` or docs for details
- Must be neutral and concise

## Implementation
- Add fallback formatting in hook/CLI output when explanation is missing
- Expose fallback in explain JSON output for consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- No empty explanation sections in any output
- Fallback never blocks output rendering
- Works with both TTY and non-TTY formats

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.3","title":"[Explain] Make explanation optional + preserve backward compatibility","description":"# Task: Make explanation optional + preserve backward compatibility

## Purpose
Ensure adding explanations does **not** break existing packs or call sites.
Patterns should function normally even without an explanation field.

## Requirements
- Explanation field is optional in `DestructivePattern`
- Default behavior: no compile/runtime failure if missing
- Output layer uses fallback explanation when missing

## Implementation
- Update `DestructivePattern` to carry `Option<&'static str>` explanation
- Update constructors/macros to allow absence
- Ensure serialization/tracing handles None cleanly

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing packs compile without modification
- Explanation output renders even when field missing (via fallback task)
